findings.title: LEAVE handler trusts self-asserted steamId in payload -> any peer force-disconnects any other peer
findings.detail: HandleLeave decodes peerSteamId from the PACKET PAYLOAD (DeserializeLeave(msg.Payload)) and, on the host, broadcasts ClientLeave for that id and RemoveClient(peerSteamId) -- the transport-authenticated sender (msg.SenderSteamId, overwritten with the real id at NetworkEngine.cs:534) is never consulted. Every peer's SteamId is public via PEER_LIST, so a malicious peer sends a ClientLeave carrying any victim's id and the host evicts that victim (drop from roster + broadcast their leave). Pure spoofed-actor authorization bypass. The sibling HandleRename/HandleChat already key off msg.SenderSteamId; HandleLeave is the lone un-hardened handler. verify-gate CONFIRMED (survives adversarial re-read; RemoveClient runs unconditionally, IsHost only gates the re-broadcast).
findings.snippet:
var peerSteamId = MessageSerializer.DeserializeLeave(msg.Payload);
if (_engine.IsHost) { _engine.BroadcastToAll(... SerializeLeave(peerSteamId)); } RemoveClient(peerSteamId);
findings.recommendation: On the host, ignore the payload id and key the leave off msg.SenderSteamId (the transport-authenticated sender), exactly as HandleRename/HandleChat do. A client may only leave itself.

findings.title: Phase-1 straggler kick strands a slow-but-alive client on the load curtain (and can re-admit it as an untracked ghost)
findings.detail: After OpenBarrier, if a connected client has not acked LOADED within Phase1LoadTimeoutMs (180s), the host Update() loop kicks it via Session.RemoveClient(clientId) then Begin(). RemoveClient (SessionManager.cs:214-226) does NOT disconnect the peer at transport and sends NO terminal notice. Two outcomes, both bad: (a) a genuinely slow straggler still in PrepareEntryFromBlobCrt drops the early SessionBegin (delivered at transport but arriving before _pendingResult exists and NOT latched), so IsBarrierPending stays true forever -> it sits behind the held load curtain permanently; its late LOADED ack is a silent no-op (TryReleaseBarrier early-returns on !_barrierOpen); IsBarrierPending keeps loadInFlight true so its host-heartbeat timeout is suspended -> only a manual Leave escapes (NEW-1/reproduction verdict REPRODUCED, adj-conf 88). (b) If the straggler HAD finished prepare and was only late on LOADED, the transport-delivered SessionBegin drives it INTO the level while the host removed it from the roster -> a desynced, un-commandable ghost (intents fail-closed via ResolveActor->Guid.Empty, absent from AllDone accounting).
findings.snippet:
foreach (var clientId in stragglers) { _engine.Session.RemoveClient(clientId); } // no transport Disconnect
Begin(); // reliable SessionBegin still fans out to the still-transport-connected kicked peer
findings.recommendation: On a straggler kick, force a transport-level Disconnect of the peer (or send a terminal ConnectionRejected/OnClientTransferFailed) BEFORE Begin(), so the kicked peer neither strands behind the curtain nor enters as a ghost. Keep roster removal and transport state consistent.
findings.repro_note: static-confirmed: reproduction-agent traced the full RemoveClient/Begin/EnterLevel/TryReleaseBarrier path at HEAD (REPRODUCED, adjusted_confidence 88); end-state hang not runtime-observed (no game DLLs).

findings.title: Unbounded payload length in outer frame decoder -> remote memory-exhaustion DoS
findings.detail: NetworkMessage.Deserialize reads payloadLen = BitConverter.ToInt32(buffer,33) from the wire with NO upper bound, then allocates `new byte[payloadLen]` (line 71) BEFORE the Array.Copy presence check (line 72). payloadLen is fully attacker-controlled: a ~37-byte packet can declare up to int.MaxValue. On net472 `new byte[n]` commits n bytes eagerly, so mid-range declared lengths (hundreds of MB) allocate repeatedly -> sustained GC pressure / OOM. This is the OUTERMOST decoder, run for EVERY inbound packet from ANY connected peer BEFORE auth/routing; OnPacketReceived's try/catch cannot undo the transient commit. Same unbounded-alloc class recurs in SEC-004/006/007. verify-gate CONFIRMED (only the crash-vs-pressure end-state is runtime-unverified).
findings.snippet:
var payloadLen = BitConverter.ToInt32(buffer, offset); offset += 4;
if (payloadLen > 0) { msg.Payload = new byte[payloadLen]; Array.Copy(buffer, offset, msg.Payload, 0, payloadLen); }
findings.recommendation: Reject before allocating: require payloadLen >= 0 && payloadLen <= (buffer.Length - 37), and cap at a sane transport max (e.g. 8 MB). Drop the packet on violation.

findings.title: Client save-reassembly leaks _rxTotalBytes>0 on a faulting-allocation transfer, permanently pinning heartbeat-suspension and stranding the client
findings.detail: OnSaveChunk sizes the reassembly buffer straight from the first chunk's network-supplied length (`_rxTotalBytes = chunk.TotalBytes; _rxBuffer = new byte[chunk.TotalBytes]`, lines 991-992) with NO bound. A positive-but-allocation-faulting TotalBytes (OOM / >int.MaxValue overflow) throws AT the allocation AFTER _rxTotalBytes was assigned, so _rxBuffer stays null; the throw is swallowed by OnPacketReceived. When SaveDone arrives, the unknown-transfer guard (`_rxBuffer==null`, line 1073) does SendLoaded(false)+return WITHOUT ResetRx (unlike the incomplete and CRC-mismatch branches below it, which both ResetRx). So _rxTotalBytes stays >0 -> TransferActive/IsDownloading stay true -> SessionManager keeps loadInFlight true and SKIPS the client-side host-heartbeat + half-open detector every tick -> the client can no longer detect a dead host and never enters the level. Reproduction refined the trigger: only the LARGE-POSITIVE (fault-on-alloc) case pins (negative leaves _rxTotalBytes<0 so TransferActive is false); real on the best-effort Stun/WAN path or from a malicious host (REPRODUCED, adj-conf 85).
findings.snippet: if (_rxBuffer == null || transferId != _rxTransferId) { Debug.LogError(...); SendLoaded(transferId, false); return; } // <-- no ResetRx(); _rxTotalBytes stays > 0
findings.recommendation: Bound-check chunk.TotalBytes (0 < TotalBytes <= a sane cap, e.g. 64 MB) before allocating, aborting the transfer + AbortDownloadCurtain on violation; and add ResetRx() to the OnSaveDone unknown-transfer early-return so a null/mismatched buffer can never leave _rxTotalBytes>0.
findings.repro_note: static-confirmed: reproduction-agent traced OnSaveChunk/OnSaveDone/TransferActive at HEAD (REPRODUCED, adjusted_confidence 85; trigger narrowed to positive fault-on-alloc TotalBytes); heartbeat-pin end-state not runtime-observed.

findings.title: Identity takeover via spoofed PlayerGuid in JOIN -> evict victim + inherit their slot/permissions/owned soldiers
findings.detail: Player identity/ownership is keyed on the persistent PlayerGuid, which the joining client asserts in JOIN and which is NOT secret (broadcast to all peers in PEER_LIST). HandleConnectionRequest rejects only Guid.Empty and the host's own guid. A JOIN carrying ANOTHER connected player's PlayerGuid is treated as that player 'reconnecting': StaleRejoinPeers returns every roster peer whose guid==joiningGuid REGARDLESS of steamId (by design, for reconnect over a different transport address), the host RemoveClient()s the victim (line 356) and rebinds the attacker's transport id to the victim's guid (client.PlayerGuid = join.PlayerGuid, line 395), granting FullCommander keyed by that guid. ResolveActor(attackerPeerId) now returns the victim's guid -> attacker controls the victim's soldiers/slot/flags. No SteamId<->PlayerGuid binding exists. verify-gate CONFIRMED (conf<90 only because the attacker-issues-commands end-state is static-traced).
findings.snippet:
foreach (var staleId in SessionLifecycle.StaleRejoinPeers(GetClientIdentities(), join.PlayerGuid)) { RemoveClient(staleId); ... }
// then: client.PlayerGuid = join.PlayerGuid;
findings.recommendation: Bind PlayerGuid to the authenticated transport identity: on first JOIN record SteamId<->PlayerGuid; on a reconnect (matching guid) require the new connection's msg.SenderSteamId to equal the stored SteamId, else reject. Do not let a peer claim a guid held by a different live SteamId.

findings.title: tac.actorstate (0x8F) client apply: one throwing actor aborts the whole batch suffix and skips the seq Mark -> durable silent per-actor desync
findings.detail: HandleActorState iterates `foreach (var rec in batch.Actors)` (line 325) calling SetApWpAbsolute/ApplyHealthMirror/ApplyBodyPartHp -> SetStat (1397) / GetProp/GetField (1436/1445) whose Invoke()/GetValue() do NOT swallow exceptions. BaseStat.Set(value, triggerStatChangeEvent:true) fires native StatChangeEvent whose subscribers (healthbar/AI/TFTV) can throw on a frozen mirror actor (the codebase already documents mirror applies NRE-ing -- see the per-status try/catch at 1043). Unlike ReconcileStatuses, the per-ACTOR body has NO try/catch; LiveSeq.Mark (403) is inside the try AFTER the loop, so a throw unwinds the foreach, hits only finally{_applyingRemote=false} then the outer catch (410) -- Mark is SKIPPED. Every actor after the poison actor never gets its AP/WP/HP/pos/facing, and because Mark never advances, _clientLast[TacActorState] freezes and every later flush re-aborts at the same actor -> durable silent desync of the batch suffix for the rest of the mission. Sibling appliers TacticalStructDamageSync (try/catch 181-194, Mark 199) and TacticalSurfaceSync (161/167) already use the correct per-record-guard + Mark-after pattern; HandleActorState is the lone deviation. Concrete instance of the HIST-001 reflection-fragility family, compounded by IMP-003 (no divergence probe on the tactical rail). verify-gate CONFIRMED (conf 70: guard omission certain, the throw trigger is conditional).
findings.snippet: foreach (var rec in batch.Actors) { ... SetApWpAbsolute(actor, rec); ApplyHealthMirror(actor, rec.Health); ApplyBodyPartHp(actor, rec.BodyParts); } // no per-actor try/catch; LiveSeq.Mark(TacActorState, batch.Seq) at line 403 is after the loop
findings.recommendation: Wrap the per-actor body in its own try/catch that logs netId+exception and continues, exactly as TacticalStructDamageSync/TacticalSurfaceSync do. Keep LiveSeq.Mark after the loop so the seq still advances (a poison actor is then skipped each tick but the rest of the batch and all later batches converge).

findings.title: Stale comments assert status-mirror is GATED OFF (SyncStatuses==false) while it is ON; dead IsSyncableStatusType allowlist contradicts the 'no allowlist' canon
findings.detail: The comment block 330-368 states the status mirror is disabled ('Status sync stays GATED OFF (TacticalActorStateSync.SyncStatuses == false)', 'no status reaches the wire', 'TODO: vetted allowlist before enabling SyncStatuses') and presents AllowedStatusTypeNames/IsSyncableStatusType as the ACTIVE gate. Contradicted by code: TacticalActorStateSync.cs:56 'internal const bool SyncStatuses = true;' -- statuses ARE mirrored live via ShouldMirrorStatus (:149). The allowlist is DEAD: IsSyncableStatusType/AllowedStatusTypeNames have NO production caller (only doc <see cref> + 3 unit tests). Keeping a live-compiled dead allowlist that directly contradicts the enforced 'no allowlist' sync-canon (CLAUDE.md + design S5) is a parallel-implementation smell that misleads a maintainer into reading the exact opposite of runtime behavior. Root cause: commit 4053418 (Feature B) flipped SyncStatuses=true and updated TacticalActorStateSync.cs but NOT this file. reproduction verified TRUE via full-repo grep (const=true, zero production callers).
findings.snippet: // Status sync stays GATED OFF (TacticalActorStateSync.SyncStatuses == false) ... [reality: SyncStatuses == true, statuses mirrored via ShouldMirrorStatus]
findings.recommendation: Rewrite/delete the 330-368 allowlist commentary to state the live policy (SyncStatuses==true; mirror = ShouldMirrorStatus visibility gate + SurfaceOwnedStatusTypeNames exclude + ClientStatusMirrorGuards inertness). Delete the dead IsSyncableStatusType/AllowedStatusTypeNames + their unit tests, or mark obsolete. Drop the obsolete TODO at :352.
findings.repro_note: static-confirmed: reproduction-agent (CMT-001/CONV-002 are one issue) verified the stale comments + dead allowlist TRUE by grep (adjusted_confidence 95).

findings.title: File-header describes IsSyncableStatusType as an INCLUDE policy that syncs Panic/stat-mods and excludes DoT -- self-contradicts its own body and live behavior
findings.detail: Class-header item 2 (L17-23) says IsSyncableStatusType 'T1 syncs buffs/debuffs/stances/disables (Paralysed/Frenzy/Shield/Panic/Silenced/stat-mods)' and EXCLUDES only Overwatch + the DoT family. This contradicts (a) the same file's body L336-343 which lists PanicStatus/StatsModifyStatus as KNOWN-UNSAFE types that 'must NEVER be added to the allowlist', and (b) live behavior: the DoT family IS mirrored (icons), per ShouldMirrorStatus + the note at L116 'The DoT family is deliberately NOT excluded'. Two mutually-exclusive descriptions of the same dead method plus a false statement about live DoT handling, on the sync-critical status path.
findings.snippet: header L17-23: 'T1 syncs ...Panic...stat-mods...; EXCLUDES ...the DAMAGE-OVER-TIME family' vs body L338: 'PanicStatus...StatsModifyStatus...must NEVER be added' & L116: 'DoT family is deliberately NOT excluded'
findings.recommendation: Update the class-header item 2 to match the live ShouldMirrorStatus policy (visibility-gated inert icon mirror; excludes only Overwatch/MindControl/Zombified; DoT icons INCLUDED), or remove the item if IsSyncableStatusType is deleted per FA-0007.

findings.title: Dead class: SavePickerPanel (628 LOC, zero references) ships in the assembly
findings.detail: SavePickerPanel is a complete mod-drawn uGUI save-picker class that is never instantiated, never referenced from product code or tests, and has no [HarmonyPatch] attribute (Serena find_referencing_symbols empty; grep shows only the declaration). Referenced only in design docs as a planned feature never wired up. 628 lines of dead code compiled into the final assembly.
findings.recommendation: Delete src/UI/SavePickerPanel.cs. Also remove the UnityEngine.ImageConversionModule csproj reference (FA-0041), its only consumer. Rebuild from the design spec if the feature is ever needed.

findings.title: Mod uses deprecated SteamNetworking P2P API instead of SteamNetworkingSockets (weaker spoofing protection, no built-in encryption)
findings.detail: The transport uses Steamworks.SteamNetworking (old P2P: SendP2PPacket/OnP2PSessionRequest/AcceptP2PSessionWithUser). Valve deprecated this in favor of SteamNetworkingSockets/ISteamNetworkingMessages (end-to-end encryption, SDR relay, better identity verification). The old API has weaker spoofing protection and no built-in encryption. Migration is BLOCKED: the mod is constrained to the API surface the game's shipped Facepunch DLL exposes. Weak-authentication of the P2P channel is the transport-level backdrop for the identity/authorization findings (SEC-002/003).
findings.recommendation: Old P2P lacks built-in encryption; the mod already layers its own framing. Consider message authentication (HMAC) over the P2P channel. Migration to SteamNetworkingSockets is blocked by the game-shipped DLL version -- document the constraint.

findings.title: Facepunch.Steamworks game-shipped DLL is behind latest (2.3.x vs 2.5.2)
findings.detail: The mod references the game-shipped Facepunch.Steamworks.Win64.dll (Private=false, from GameManaged). NuGet last published 2.3.3 (Feb 2020); GitHub latest is 2.5.2 (Apr 2024). The game bundles an unknown 2.3.x. 2.5.x adds SteamNetworkingSockets support and fixes. The mod cannot upgrade independently (uses the game's DLL) but the gap means missing upstream fixes.
findings.recommendation: No action possible -- version locked to the game-shipped DLL. Document the constraint; re-evaluate if Phoenix Point updates their Facepunch DLL.

findings.title: Unbounded collection counts + length in parity-manifest / JOIN decode -> alloc/CPU DoS on host
findings.detail: DeserializeParityManifest reads dlcCount/modCount/setCount/entryCount as raw Int32 (no upper bound) and loops ReadString() that many times; DeserializeJoin reads the manifest block length as Int32 then br.ReadBytes(len). On net472 ReadBytes(count) allocates `new byte[count]` eagerly, so a crafted JOIN (routed to host via ConnectionRequest) with len ~2e9 forces a multi-hundred-MB/2GB transient allocation, and huge count fields drive an allocate-and-throw loop. Reachable by any peer that can send a ConnectionRequest. Same root cause as SEC-001.
findings.snippet:
var dlcCount = br.ReadInt32(); for (var i=0;i<dlcCount;i++) m.Dlc.Add(br.ReadString());
// DeserializeJoin: var len = br.ReadInt32(); msg.Manifest = DeserializeParityManifest(br.ReadBytes(len));
findings.recommendation: Bound every wire-supplied count/length against the remaining stream length before allocating/looping (reject count > remainingBytes; cap manifest block len to a few KB). Prefer u16 length prefixes as the rest of SyncProtocol already does.

findings.title: Reflection version-fragility: ~1342 AccessTools sites, only ~19 startup-guarded; unresolved lookups degrade to silent per-site desync
findings.detail: Recurring bug class across history and the parent of the reflection-fragility family. The mod reaches PP/TFTV internals through ~1342 AccessTools.Field/Property/Method call-sites (census at HEAD across 139 files) but the startup ReflectionGuard.Critical list covers only ~19 curated bindings. At the deref sites the cached member is used inside per-item try/catch that swallows and continues (e.g. GeoSiteReflection.cs:321 'catch { continue; }'), so a PP/TFTV rename/removal that nulls a lookup becomes a SILENT per-site skip = the exact 'mid-game DESYNC instead of a clear error' the mod's own doc warns about. History proves the class keeps biting: 57e98bb/99f6435 guard added vs renames, and two member-KIND mismatches shipped as data loss (e2f9744 items destroyed on unequip; 602ceac client gestures relay again). reproduction verified all three structural claims TRUE (census, ~19 guards, catch{continue} silent-skip); the harmful desync is version-drift-conditional so it cannot be runtime-reproduced without a mismatched game assembly.
findings.snippet: try { id = _siteIdField.GetValue(s) is int i ? i : -1; } catch { continue; } // unguarded reflected member, failure = silent skip
findings.recommendation: Extend the curated ReflectionGuard.Critical set to every LOAD-BEARING binding on the sync spine (the pillars whose silent loss desyncs co-op), so a PP/TFTV update fails LOUD at host/join. Cheaper: a build/CI reflection-manifest test that resolves each declared binding against the shipped assemblies. Do NOT rely on per-site try/catch{continue} as the drift signal -- it hides the drift.
findings.repro_note: static-confirmed: reproduction-agent verified census (1342 sites/139 files), 19 curated guards, and the catch{continue} silent-skip pattern at HEAD (REPRODUCED-structural, adjusted_confidence 68; desync is version-drift-conditional, held below the runtime floor).

findings.title: Per-soldier ownership gate is vacuous under the blanket FullCommander grant -> any peer edits any player's soldiers (IDOR / false assurance)
findings.detail: The personnel-edit intents advertise a per-soldier ownership check: Validate() -> OwnsSoldier -> CanControlSoldier, which short-circuits to true whenever the actor holds CampaignPermission.FullCommander (line 85). HandleConnectionRequest grants EVERY joiner FullCommander (SessionManager.cs:407), so OwnsSoldier returns true for ANY unitId -- the wired 'per-soldier ownership' gate enforces nothing (any peer can dismiss/augment/transfer/rename/edit-stats on any soldier). verify-gate CONFIRMED as a FALSE-ASSURANCE / latent-gap finding, NOT an active cross-trust exploit: the blanket grant is documented current co-op policy (all peers trusted, one shared faction), so it is intended today. The defect is a security gate that provides zero protection and nothing (test/assert) prevents FullCommander-by-default if a per-owner/PS4 mode ships.
findings.snippet: if (HasFlag(assignment.Permissions, CampaignPermission.FullCommander)) return true; // short-circuits OwnsSoldier
findings.recommendation: Do not treat OwnsSoldier as security while FullCommander is granted to all. When a per-player model ships, gate FullCommander behind an explicit host toggle and add a test asserting a non-owner is rejected. For trusted-friend co-op today, document that peers can edit each other's soldiers by design.

findings.title: Actor registry keyed by IActorRef with no value-equality on TacticalActorAdapter -> AssignHost idempotency / NetIdOf / RemoveActor silently broken (latent duplicate-mint)
findings.detail: _netIdByActor is keyed on IActorRef, but TacticalActorAdapter overrides NEITHER Equals NOR GetHashCode, and every call site constructs a fresh `new TacticalActorAdapter(actor)`. So two adapters wrapping the SAME live actor are unequal keys: (1) AssignHost's idempotency guard TryGetValue(actor) always misses -> a second AssignHost for a Pandoran (GeoUnitId==0) MINTS A NEW netId (double-mint -> client binds two netIds to one actor / phantom); (2) NetIdOf/RemoveActor can never find a fresh adapter. Currently MASKED (HostEnsureLiveActorsRegistered dedups by wrapped-actor reference before AssignHost; NetIdForLiveActor scans Entries by ReferenceEquals; RemoveActor/NetIdOf have zero callers) so no active desync, but the registry's public API is a silent trap for any future caller trusting AssignHost idempotency or calling RemoveActor on death. Identity-fragility sibling of HIST-001.
findings.snippet: private readonly Dictionary<IActorRef,int> _netIdByActor = new(); // TacticalActorAdapter (TacticalDeploySync.cs:1937) has no Equals/GetHashCode, always new-ed per lookup
findings.recommendation: Either give TacticalActorAdapter value-equality delegating to ReferenceEquals(_actor, other._actor) + RuntimeHelpers.GetHashCode, OR key _netIdByActor on the wrapped actor object (Dictionary<object,int> reference identity) so AssignHost/NetIdOf/RemoveActor are correct-by-construction and the reference-scan workarounds can be deleted.

findings.title: Unbounded chunk count / totalLen in tactical deploy reassembler -> alloc bomb (host->client)
findings.detail: TacticalDeployChunkCodec.TryDecode reads count and totalLen as raw Int32 and validates only count<=0/totalLen<0 -- no upper bound. ChunkReassembler.Accept then allocates `new byte[count][]`, `new bool[count]`, and `new byte[totalLen]` sized directly by the wire values. A single crafted tac.deploy chunk with count~2e9 forces a ~16GB jagged-array allocation; totalLen~2e9 forces ~2GB. Host->all surface, so the attacker is a malicious/compromised host targeting clients -- a clean OOM-crash vector. Envelope u16 length capping bounds fragment bytes but NOT the count/totalLen header fields.
findings.snippet: int count = r.ReadInt32(); int totalLen = r.ReadInt32(); ... if (count <= 0 || idx < 0 || idx >= count || totalLen < 0) return false;
findings.recommendation: Cap count and totalLen to sane maxima (count <= a few thousand; totalLen <= max save-blob size) in TryDecode before returning a Fragment, and reject fragments whose totalLen/count are inconsistent across the set.

findings.title: SSRF: WebRequest.Create with URL from SSDP LAN discovery (HttpGet)
findings.detail: HttpGet() passes a URL parsed from an SSDP M-SEARCH response directly to WebRequest.Create(). The URL originates from a LAN broadcast response (UPnP router discovery), not web-facing user input, so a malicious device on the same LAN could craft an SSDP response pointing to an internal service. Severity reduced from the semgrep ERROR because the source is LAN-scoped SSDP discovery.
findings.snippet: private static string HttpGet(string url){ try { var req = (HttpWebRequest)WebRequest.Create(url);
findings.recommendation: Validate the URL scheme (http/https only) and optionally restrict to RFC1918 addresses before WebRequest.Create(). Low priority given LAN-only UPnP context.

findings.title: SSRF: WebRequest.Create with URL from SSDP LAN discovery (SoapPost)
findings.detail: SoapPost() passes a controlUrl parsed from the router's device XML to WebRequest.Create(). Same LAN-scoped trust boundary as FA-0017: a crafted SSDP response could redirect this POST to an arbitrary LAN endpoint.
findings.snippet: private static string SoapPost(string controlUrl, ...){ ... var req = (HttpWebRequest)WebRequest.Create(controlUrl);
findings.recommendation: Same as FA-0017: validate URL scheme and restrict to LAN addresses.

findings.title: Client unconditionally suppresses every IHostOnlyApply event-answer outcome; non-channelled geoscape event outcomes permanently desync
findings.detail: On every client, OnActionApply consumes the seq then, because `action is IHostOnlyApply`, RETURNS before applying ANY outcome side-effect -- relying entirely on separate state channels (wallet/research/inventory/GeoSite/Diplomacy) to re-converge the consequences. The code's OWN TODO (lines 523-526) names four outcome kinds it believes are NOT re-converged -- 'site reveal / mission spawn / faction-diplomacy flag / direct research unlock'. Any such outcome leaves the client's geoscape permanently diverged from the host (a mission/site the host sees and the client does not) with NO reconvergence until a save-reload boundary reseeds a fresh blob. comment-checker (CMT-004) confirms the TODO is accurate and current (added 2026-07-05); adversarial-hunt confirms the suppression is unconditional and code-verified. Complements the dirty-mark-bypass class (HIST-S01, suspected) from the opposite end.
findings.snippet:
if (action is IHostOnlyApply) { // TODO(multiplayer): non-channelled event outcomes (site reveal / mission spawn / faction-diplomacy flag / direct research unlock) are NOT yet synced to the client; visible gap.
    Debug.Log(...); return; }
findings.recommendation: Enumerate every geoscape EVENT outcome kind and prove each is covered by a state channel (or drift-poll) on the client; for any outcome with no channel backstop (candidate: an event that spawns a mission site or sets a diplomacy flag), add an explicit sync (channel echo or dedicated outcome push) rather than suppress-and-hope. At minimum resolve the code's own TODO by mapping each of the four named kinds to its channel or filing them as known unsynced.

findings.title: Canon invariant 5 (outcome-at-impact-frame) enforced by co-timed broadcast, not a true ordering barrier
findings.detail: Canon inv.5 requires 'one action = ONE synchronized causal beat (shot->impact->status), outcome applied at impact frame, never stat-before-anim.' The implementation resolves this by CO-TIMING two independent surfaces: the host fires tac.fire.start (0x90) and tac.damage (0x88) both from the same host shot-coroutine prefix so they arrive in lock-step. There is NO client-side cross-surface barrier holding the damage apply (TacticalCombatSync.HandleDamage, :945) until the fire animation reaches its impact frame -- the two surfaces remain independent envelopes with independent TacticalLiveSeq. So 'outcome at the presentation's impact frame' is APPROXIMATED (arrive-together), not structurally guaranteed. The codebase's own Inc3 design doc flags exactly this as unverified ('does the client ever show impact/HP-drop BEFORE the shot animation plays?'). Tracked as Inc3 roadmap 'NEXT' -> incremental-convergence debt, hence MEDIUM (a stricter reading of 'canon violation = HIGH minimum' would raise it).
findings.recommendation: Complete Inc3: add the explicit client-side impact-frame ordering barrier (queue the tac.damage apply behind the fire.start replay's impact callback) instead of relying on co-timed arrival, then in-game-verify HP never drops before the shot anim. Until then keep the Inc3 risk item open.

findings.title: Verification debt concentrated in the reflection glue: shipping depth outruns in-game verification (dominant purpose-fit risk)
findings.detail: The architecture deliberately splits pure BCL cores (unit-tested, ~2258 tests green) from engine glue that is reflection-bound -> NOT linked -> in-game-gated. Correct separation, BUT the layer that actually exercises co-op against the real game (every *Sync.cs / *Reflection.cs) has NO automated coverage by construction; its only proof is the per-increment 2-instance in-game gate, and the project's own living tracker (docs/COOP-SYNC-ROADMAP.md) shows that gate is largely UNPAID: the Core skeleton is 'in-game UNVERIFIED', the entire tactical live-state spine (Inc1 position / Inc2 facing) is 'in-game pending', and dozens of surfaces are 'SHIPPED, pending in-game soak'. Because Inc2 rides on unverified Inc1 on an unverified generic spine, verification risk COMPOUNDS and green unit tests cannot detect a defect in the reflected apply. This is the single biggest gap between roadmap PROMISES and shipped REALITY, and it is why the canon convergence (Inc6 dual-rail retirement) is STALLED behind the in-game-proof queue.
findings.recommendation: Treat the in-game soak backlog as release-blocking, not a trailing chore: drain the tactical-spine gate (Inc1 position, Inc2 facing) BEFORE layering further fields, and gate new surface work behind soak of its base. Add a thin repeatable in-game smoke harness (the tracker already runs 2-instance DirectIP) so the glue layer gets recurring verification. This unblocks the tracked Inc6 convergence.

findings.title: Tactical 0x67 actor-state rail has no divergence probe and relies on symmetric per-mission seq reset; a one-sided skew silently drops the whole rail
findings.detail: The shared SurfaceSeq guard is a strict `seq > last` (SurfaceSeq.cs:38). The tactical actor-state stream (0x8F on the 0x67 rail) is authored from TacticalDeploySync.LiveSeq and reset only at OnMissionExit (BeginDeployCaptureMission is a deliberate no-op -- must NOT rewind mid-mission). Correctness therefore depends on BOTH host _hostNext AND every client _clientLast being reset symmetrically at each mission boundary. If a client's _clientLast skews above the host's fresh _hostNext (a missed OnMissionExit or a reload-boundary asymmetry), HandleActorState's ShouldApply drops the NEXT mission's entire actor-state batch stream -- AP/WP/health/position/facing/ammo/status mirror all silently stop converging. Unlike the geoscape rail (Inc5 CrcProbeMirror + DivergenceMonitor + reload grace), the tactical rail has NO divergence detector, so a seq skew is a silent recovery-less desync with no alarm. Entry paths are gated (mid-battle join rejected) so both sides normally enter each mission together -- latent, not routine (reproduction SKIPPED_RUNTIME; structural facts confirmed, trigger needs a broken invariant that can't be induced statically). Compounds LOG-001.
findings.snippet: public bool ShouldApply(ushort surfaceId, uint seq){ _clientLast.TryGetValue(surfaceId, out var last); return seq > last; } // strict
findings.recommendation: Either carry a per-mission epoch/id alongside the seq (so a client resets _clientLast when the mission epoch changes rather than trusting an out-of-band OnMissionExit), or extend the Inc5 CRC divergence probe to a tactical subset so a stalled actor-state rail is at least detected + surfaced.

findings.title: 454 whitespace formatting violations across 27 files
findings.detail: dotnet format --verify-no-changes reports 454 WHITESPACE errors across 27 source files (inconsistent indentation, tabs vs spaces). Top affected: CrcProbeMirror.cs, MarketplaceReflection.cs, SyncEngine.cs, SessionManager.cs, GeoAbilityRelayReflection.cs.
findings.recommendation: Run 'dotnet format' to auto-fix; add a format check to CI.

findings.title: Three stale merged feature branches not cleaned up
findings.detail: Branches feat/coop-loading-overlay, feat/geoscape-command-sync, feat/native-coop-lobby are fully merged into main (495-533 commits behind) but not deleted; last commits 2026-06-11..13.
findings.recommendation: Delete: git branch -d feat/coop-loading-overlay feat/geoscape-command-sync feat/native-coop-lobby

findings.title: Hardcoded machine-specific absolute paths in tracked script
findings.detail: Lines 33-38 hardcode one developer's machine paths (MAIN_INSTALL=D:\Steam\..., STEAM_GAME_DIR, WORKSHOP_DIR, GB_SRC=E:\DEV\PhoenixPoint\...). They break for any other contributor.
findings.recommendation: Read paths from environment variables or a gitignored .env/.ini file with documented defaults.

findings.title: .NET Framework 4.7.2 is supported but behind latest (4.8.1)
findings.detail: .NET Framework 4.7.2 is still actively supported (lifecycle tied to the host Windows OS). Latest is 4.8.1 (Aug 2022). The mod targets net472 because Phoenix Point (Unity/Mono) requires it -- not a free choice. No security concern.
findings.recommendation: No action needed. net472 is supported and required by the game runtime; cannot upgrade independently.

findings.title: Stale XML doc-comment: 'no ammo-sync surface built' -- one was built later (TS5)
findings.detail: FireAmmoChargeNeuterPatch summary (L141-147) states client ammo 'stays host-authoritative, frozen at the snapshot value until a future ammo-sync surface (TODO - NOT built here)'. That surface now EXISTS: TS5 ammo (0x0400 on the 0x8F actor-state spine) was added 2026-07-06 (commit 91ba6f8); per-weapon magazine charges ride the actor-state signature and re-broadcast on reload/fire. The neuter patch itself is still correct (prevents the replay draining before the authoritative value arrives), but the forward-reference is stale.
findings.recommendation: Amend the parenthetical: ammo is now incrementally synced via the TS5 0x0400 actor-state bit; the neuter only prevents the local replay from double-decrementing before that broadcast lands. Remove the 'TODO - NOT built here'.

findings.title: Hardcoded developer SteamID64 in tracked script
findings.detail: Line 107 sets REAL_ID=76561197996210591 -- the developer's personal Steam account ID. Semi-public but a personal identifier committed to source, used to compute per-instance unique IDs for test copies.
findings.recommendation: Move to an environment variable or a gitignored local config file; fall back to a placeholder with a clear error.

findings.title: No .gitattributes -- line-ending normalization not configured
findings.detail: No .gitattributes exists. All sampled .cs files currently use LF consistently, but without explicit normalization multi-platform contributors could introduce CRLF inconsistencies (common on Windows C# projects).
findings.recommendation: Add .gitattributes: * text=auto eol=lf / *.cs text eol=lf / *.bat text eol=crlf / *.sln text eol=crlf

findings.title: Canon design doc's '0x91 melee = STUB' is a STALE doc, not a real capability gap (melee is fully shipped)
findings.detail: The canon design doc S2 fragmentation table and the CLAUDE.md sync-canon bullet describe melee as '0x91 melee = STUB' / asymmetric with fire. CODE REALITY: melee is a COMPLETE symmetric presentation surface -- TacticalMeleeAnimSync.ClientOnMeleeStart (:98) fully replays the native BashAbility.BashCrt swing coroutine (damage-less, no-return-fire, ammo-stable) driven via reflected MethodInfo exactly like fire, and HostBroadcastMeleeStart (:63) emits 0x91 gated to BashAbility. So the 'STUB' descriptor is a point-in-time S2 snapshot that incremental convergence has overtaken. Classification: STALE DOC, not a shipped capability gap. Confirms the comment-checker suspected item CMT-S01 by reading the code.
findings.recommendation: Refresh the canon design S2 fragmentation snapshot (and any CLAUDE.md 'melee STUB' framing) to state melee 0x91 is now a full symmetric replay. Bundle with the CONV-003 (FA-0035) canon-doc refresh in one doc pass.

findings.title: GitHub Actions: mutable action tag (actions/checkout@v7)
findings.detail: actions/checkout@v7 uses a mutable major-version tag instead of a pinned SHA. A compromised or force-pushed tag could inject malicious code into CI.
findings.recommendation: Pin to a full SHA hash (actions/checkout@<sha>). Standard supply-chain hardening.

findings.title: GitHub Actions: mutable action tag (actions/setup-dotnet@v5)
findings.detail: actions/setup-dotnet@v5 uses a mutable major-version tag instead of a pinned SHA.
findings.recommendation: Pin to a full SHA hash.

findings.title: Diagnostic-only Harmony patch ships in production (168 LOC)
findings.detail: AbilityBarStateDiagPatch is documented 'HOST-only DIAGNOSTIC (no behaviour change)'; it logs ability-bar state every time UIModuleAbilities.SetAbilities fires on the host. It has [HarmonyPatch] so PatchAll() loads it. Its docstring says it is instrumentation for a bug investigation, not a fix -- debug logging in production, ~168 LOC of overhead every host co-op session.
findings.recommendation: Gate behind #if DEBUG or a runtime config flag, or remove once the ability-bar re-cycle bug is fixed.

findings.title: HarmonyLib (0Harmony) version unknown, latest is 2.4.2 (no known CVEs)
findings.detail: The mod references 0Harmony.dll from ModSDK (Private=false) with no version specified. Latest NuGet Lib.Harmony is 2.4.2 (Nov 2025), targets net472. No CVEs for Lib.Harmony (pardeike/Harmony). The ModSDK-shipped version is unknown and cannot be controlled by the mod.
findings.recommendation: No CVEs known; version locked to ModSDK. Document which Harmony version the ModSDK ships (check 0Harmony.dll file version).

findings.title: Enforced canon spec stale: says hard-exclude faction-flippers, silent on the new display-only faction mirror
findings.detail: Canon design S5.4 (enforced spec) reads 'HARD-EXCLUDE faction-flippers (MindControl, Zombified) even when visible'. The status mirror DOES hard-exclude them (SurfaceOwnedStatusTypeNames, :120-125) -- compliant. BUT a newer mechanism (TS5 b) re-introduces the faction flip as DISPLAY: ActorFieldFaction 0x0800 on the spine + ShouldApplyFactionDisplay + ApplyFactionDisplay set TacticalFaction via the protected setter and fire only FactionChangedEvent, deliberately NOT the native SetFaction sim re-home. This is canon-compliant by construction (rides the one generic spine, host-authoritative, display-only) -- NOT a violation, but the enforced canon TEXT still reads as an absolute hard-exclude with no mention of the sanctioned display-only faction mirror, so the spec is out of date vs the code.
findings.recommendation: Update canon design S5.4 (and the CLAUDE.md status bullet) to document the faction FLIP is shown via the display-only ActorFieldFaction spine field, distinct from the hard-excluded faction-flipper STATUS. Bundle with FA-0030's doc refresh.

findings.title: Historical CVEs in Valve Game Networking Sockets (patched GNS v1.2.0, Sep 2020) -- affect the NEWER API, not the one used
findings.detail: CVE-2020-6016/6017/6018 (CVSS 9.8) and CVE-2020-6019 (7.5) were heap/stack overflows in Valve's Game Networking Sockets prior to v1.2.0. They affect the newer SteamNetworkingSockets API, NOT the old SteamNetworking P2P API this mod uses, and were all patched Sep 2020. Included for completeness since the mod operates in the Steam networking domain.
findings.recommendation: No action required -- these affect GNS (SteamNetworkingSockets), not the old P2P API. Worth noting if the mod ever migrates.

findings.title: God-class concentration; SyncEngine is the one genuine multi-responsibility hub every sync path routes through
findings.detail: Non-generated god-classes (>500 lines): SyncEngine.cs 2538, GeoSiteReflection.cs 2386, TacticalDeploySync.cs 1978, SaveTransferCoordinator.cs 1830, EventReflection.cs 1615, TacticalCombatSync.cs 1557, TacticalActorStateSync.cs 1454, PersonnelEditReflection.cs 1331, GeoUiRefresh.cs 1269. Arch verdict: mostly ACCEPTABLE cohesion debt -- the pure testable logic is already extracted into small Multiplayer.Core files, so the residual src bulk is irreducible reflection dispatch glue (high-cohesion funnel), and a wholesale split is actively risky (MEMORY dont-replace-working-architecture + reflection fragility). The ONE genuine exception is SyncEngine.cs: it mixes envelope dispatch/routing, the 5-channel drift-poll backstop counters, the ReloadBoundaryReset registry, and permission/intent wiring, and every geoscape sync path routes through it -> highest churn/merge-conflict, easiest place for a cross-cutting regression. No correctness impact today.
findings.recommendation: Do NOT force a god-class rewrite. Narrow scope to SyncEngine.cs: when next touched, lift the drift-poll backstop and the ReloadBoundaryReset registry into their own collaborators so the dispatch core shrinks to routing+authorize. Accept the cohesive *Reflection glue as-is.

findings.title: Presentation-layer symmetry (canon inv.5) achieved by copy-paste mirroring, not a shared abstraction
findings.detail: Canon inv.5 mandates presentation symmetric across fire/melee/move. The symmetry is real in behavior but implemented by DUPLICATING near-identical classes: TacticalMeleeAnimSync.cs (408 LOC) is a line-for-line mirror of TacticalFireAnimSync.cs (523 LOC) -- its own doc-comments say so ~10 times ('MELEE counterpart', 'Mirrors HostBroadcastFireStart', 'identical to InvokeStart'). Both hand-roll the same host-broadcast->seq->encode->BroadcastToAll shape, ReplayXCrt guard/refcount, InvokeStart(Timing), ResolveAbilityByGuid, GetProp/GetField; TacticalMoveSync (862 LOC) is the third parallel surface. Cost: a change to the replay-guard/target-build/start-coroutine contract must be hand-applied in 2-3 places and can silently drift. Maintainability smell, not a correctness bug; the register-once ergonomics are realized on the geoscape skeleton but NOT the tactical presentation surfaces.
findings.recommendation: Opportunistically (not urgently) extract the shared presentation-replay spine (common ReplayCrt guard/pump, InvokeStart, ResolveAbilityByGuid, GetProp/GetField helpers) so fire/melee/move differ only in their per-verb coroutine + target-build. Do it the next time the fire OR melee replay contract changes.

findings.title: LanIpResolver unused in product code (41 LOC, test-only references)
findings.detail: LanIpResolver.TryResolveLocalIPv4 is referenced ONLY by Multiplayer.Tests/LanIpResolverTests.cs; zero references from any src/ or other Multiplayer.Core file. Since Multiplayer.Core compiles into the mod assembly, this 41-line class ships as dead code. Possibly prepared for a future LAN-discovery UI.
findings.recommendation: YAGNI: delete until a LAN-discovery feature is implemented (the test file would also become orphaned).

findings.title: TFTV teardown-NRE suppression is a hand-maintained allowlist of 7 named hooks -- recurring whack-a-mole
findings.detail: TFTV geoscape-UI Harmony bodies deref GameUtl.CurrentLevel()/GeoLevelController during the between-levels teardown window and NRE -> TFTVLogger.Error popup storm. The fix is a PER-METHOD allowlist: each guard class targets one named TFTV member via string constants. History shows it grew one hook at a time (b49d7fd, 1e26a7f, 677b85f 'extend to 5 more', a48c5b6) and the code comment openly frames it as 'four more vectors found by sweeping real TFTV source'. At HEAD exactly 7 hooks are guarded; any other (or future-TFTV) geoscape-UI body reachable in the window is unguarded and re-surfaces the popup. Fragility, not a live crash (all currently-known hooks are covered).
findings.recommendation: Consider one shared guard keyed on the failure SHAPE: a Harmony finalizer over the TFTV.*Geoscape UI namespace that logs-once/swallows an NRE only while the level chain is dead (scope to the CurrentLevel-is-null window to avoid masking a genuine TFTV bug). If kept as an allowlist, note it needs re-sweeping on each TFTV update.

findings.title: UnityEngine.ImageConversionModule csproj reference only used by dead code
findings.detail: The UnityEngine.ImageConversionModule reference (line 79) provides Texture2D.LoadImage(). Its only consumer is src/UI/SavePickerPanel.cs:613, which is itself dead code (FA-0009). If SavePickerPanel is removed, this assembly reference has zero consumers.
findings.recommendation: Remove alongside SavePickerPanel (FA-0009). Standalone removal risk: zero (Private=false, compile-time only).

findings.title: Heartbeat-timeout suspension during any in-flight transfer/load disables host-loss detection for the full load window
findings.detail: SessionManager.Update suspends ALL heartbeat-timeout checking whenever loadInFlight = TransferActive || InPhase2 || LoadPhaseStarted (lines 122-131), on both host and client. If the host dies mid-transfer or during a client's phase-2 world-load, the client's heartbeat host-leave trigger and the half-open repair detector are both inert for the whole window (phase-1 up to 180s, phase-2 up to RevealDeadlineMs 180s); detection falls back to transport-level drop + per-peer self-reveal. Intentional (a big WAN transfer legitimately starves heartbeats) and bounded to ~3 min, but it COMPOUNDS IMP-002: a stalled transfer keeps loadInFlight true so the heartbeat path stays disabled exactly when the client is stuck.
findings.snippet: bool loadInFlight = st != null && (st.TransferActive || st.InPhase2 || st.LoadPhaseStarted); if (loadInFlight) { _lastHeartbeatAck = now; } else if (...) { /* host-timeout + half-open - SKIPPED while loadInFlight */ }
findings.recommendation: Bound the suspension with an absolute wall-clock ceiling (resume heartbeat-timeout checking once loadInFlight has held longer than Phase1LoadTimeoutMs + RevealDeadlineMs) so a transfer that stalls forever cannot indefinitely disable host-loss detection.

findings.title: Reassembly buffer sized directly from wire TotalBytes (host->client alloc, defense-in-depth)
findings.detail: OnSaveChunk (client-only) allocates `_rxBuffer = new byte[chunk.TotalBytes]` from the first chunk's wire-supplied Int64 TotalBytes with no upper bound. A malicious host can set TotalBytes huge -> large/overflowing allocation on clients (`new byte[long]` throws for >int.MaxValue, else commits). Subsequent Array.Copy IS bounds-checked (TryChunkIndex), so only the initial allocation is exposed. Host->client + host-authoritative + wrapped by OnPacketReceived try/catch, so severity is low. Same unbounded-length root cause as SEC-001/004/006; shares the allocation site with the HIGH heartbeat-pin bug (FA-0004).
findings.snippet: _rxTotalBytes = chunk.TotalBytes; _rxBuffer = new byte[chunk.TotalBytes];
findings.recommendation: Reject TotalBytes <= 0 or > a max-save-size cap (and > int.MaxValue) before allocating _rxBuffer.

findings.title: Wallet apply + persistent-bars refresh silently dropped during geo->tac transition; recovery path unverified
findings.detail: Commit 94de045 adds a client-only latch GeoTransitionGate.InTransition that makes WalletApplier.Apply (:35) and GeoUiRefresh persistent-bar repaint (:209) EARLY-RETURN without applying the host wallet echo, to dodge a TFTV RefreshResourceText NRE storm while the geoscape UI tears down. The early return drops the incoming target values. Both call sites document recovery as 'the drift-poll / full-wallet re-broadcast on geoscape re-entry', but a static scan of src/Network/Sync/ found no wallet-specific full re-broadcast keyed to geoscape re-entry (only per-change MarkWalletDirty). If a wallet value changes ONLY inside the ~seconds launch window and never again, the client's displayed wallet stays stale until the next wallet-affecting change. Bounded: client-side DISPLAY only (host authoritative), narrow window, gate is triple-cleared so it never sticks. Not a state-divergence bug, but the reconvergence claim in the comments is not backed by a located code path.
findings.snippet: if (State.GeoTransitionGate.InTransition) { Debug.Log("Wallet apply skipped guard=geo->tactical transition (recovered on geoscape re-entry)"); return; }
findings.recommendation: Either buffer the skipped wallet target and re-apply when GeoTransitionGate clears, or confirm+cite the geoscape-re-entry full-wallet re-broadcast the comments promise. Alternatively narrow the guard to suppress only the UI repaint (the NRE source) while still writing the underlying Wallet model.

findings.title: Regex without timeout on network-sourced input (ParseLocationFromSsdp)
findings.detail: Regex.Match on SSDP response text without a timeout. The pattern is simple and unlikely to backtrack catastrophically, but the input comes from a network socket (LAN SSDP response) and could be crafted.
findings.snippet: var m = Regex.Match(ssdpResponse, @"(?im)^\s*LOCATION\s*:\s*(\S+)\s*$");
findings.recommendation: Add a Regex timeout (e.g. TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(500)) for defense-in-depth. Low priority -- pattern is simple.

findings.title: Regex without timeout on network-sourced input (ParseExternalIp)
findings.detail: Regex.Match on SOAP response XML without a timeout (pattern '<NewExternalIPAddress>\s*(.*?)\s*</NewExternalIPAddress>', Singleline). Input comes from the router's HTTP response; the lazy quantifier reduces backtracking risk but no timeout is set.
findings.snippet: var m = Regex.Match(soapResponse, @"<NewExternalIPAddress>\s*(.*?)\s*</NewExternalIPAddress>", RegexOptions.Singleline);
findings.recommendation: Add a Regex timeout for defense-in-depth. Low priority -- lazy quantifier.

findings.title: Swallowing catch blocks (79 across 26 files) -- history shows a swallowed catch once hid a real deploy failure
findings.detail: 79 empty/near-empty catch blocks across 26 src files. Most are DELIBERATE fail-open/fail-safe consistent with the sync canon ('never throw into the game', per-status try/catch). BUT history proves the class can hide real bugs: 42f708e had to un-swallow a deploy error path that a broad catch was masking. Risk at HEAD: any empty catch that neither logs nor is provably benign fail-open converts a failure into a silent desync/skip. The actionable subset is the un-logged catches on state-applying paths (reflected SetValue/Invoke apply loops).
findings.recommendation: Audit the catch blocks on STATE-APPLYING paths in *Reflection.cs and Tactical*Sync.cs: a swallow there must at minimum log-once so a systematic apply failure is diagnosable. Leave the documented fail-open guards as-is.

findings.title: SteamTransport raises OnPeerConnected directly from the Steam session-request callback, unlike DirectTransport which marshals peer events to the main thread
findings.detail: OnSessionRequest calls OnPeerConnected -> Session.AddClient + TimeSync.OnPeerConnectedHost SYNCHRONOUSLY from the Steam callback. Inbound packets are correctly drained on the main thread via Update(), but the session-request/peer-connect path is NOT. DirectTransport deliberately marshals OnPeerConnected/OnPeerDisconnected through a _peerEventQueue drained in Update() because their handlers touch Unity APIs + shared clock state that must be main-thread. If Facepunch ever dispatches OnP2PSessionRequest off the game's RunCallbacks pump, AddClient mutating the plain _clients/_lastHeartbeat Dictionaries would race main-thread reads. Currently contained (Facepunch dispatches synchronously inside RunCallbacks which PP pumps on its main loop); the concern is the unstated dependency + the asymmetry with DirectTransport.
findings.snippet: private void OnSessionRequest(ulong remoteSteamId){ AcceptSession(remoteSteamId); _connectedPeers.Add(remoteSteamId); OnPeerConnected?.Invoke(remoteSteamId, $"Steam({remoteSteamId})"); } // direct - not marshalled
findings.recommendation: For symmetry + defense-in-depth, marshal OnSessionRequest's OnPeerConnected through the same Update()-drained queue SteamTransport already uses for packets (mirroring DirectTransport's _peerEventQueue).

findings.title: Faction-flipper hard-exclude is a 3-entry exact-simple-type-name match; a future/DLC faction-changing status not on the literal list is mirrored as an 'inert' icon that does NOT neutralize a faction flip
findings.detail: The live status mirror filter is ShouldMirrorStatus(healthBarVisibility, st.GetType().Name); a status is mirrored inert on the client unless its runtime simple type name is one of exactly {OverwatchStatus, MindControlStatus, ZombifiedStatus} (SurfaceOwnedStatusTypeNames). The exclusion is a STRINGLY-TYPED exact-name match. If a base-game patch/DLC/TFTV ever ships a NEW status (or a subclass of the flippers) that changes faction/control with VisibleOnHealthbar != Hidden and a name not on the 3-string list, it passes the visibility gate and is mirrored as an 'inert' icon -- but the inert path does NOT stop a faction flip (the code documents that MindControlStatus leaks an unconditional ActorDeathEvent subscription and ZombifiedStatus would flip faction if the Applied pre-set fails). A wrong-faction actor on the client = tactical desync far worse than a missing badge. VERIFIED LATENT: grep of decompiled base game + refs/TFTV-src found NO subclasses/new flipper today (TFTV Mindfragging resolves to the excluded base MindControlStatus). Faction-critical slice of the HIST-001 name-drift class.
findings.snippet: private static readonly HashSet<string> SurfaceOwnedStatusTypeNames = { "OverwatchStatus", "MindControlStatus", "ZombifiedStatus" }; // excluded by EXACT st.GetType().Name; a subclass/new flipper name is NOT matched
findings.recommendation: Make the faction-safety exclusion robust to name drift: (a) exclude by IS-A/interface or a faction-change/control trait rather than literal simple-name (catches subclasses + DLC flippers by shape), and/or (b) fail-SAFE for the faction path: if a mirrored status's Applied pre-set throws, abort mirroring that status entirely (generalize the abort the two named types already have).

suspected_unconfirmed.title: Dirty-mark-bypass desync class: host state mutated without dirty-marking is not synced; only some channels have a drift-poll backstop
suspected_unconfirmed.detail: Strong recurring history class (8f07d70/1bc0395/8f4bd7a/747e8aa): native/TFTV code mutates host game state WITHOUT raising the event/flush the sync relies on, so the change never ships -> client desync. Mitigation added: signature-drift POLL backstops -- but SyncEngine.cs:53 scopes it to 'the FIVE channels that otherwise rely only on events/Harmony patches' (inventory/research/unlock/objectives/recruit-pool). Channels WITHOUT a drift-poll still depend solely on dirty-mark/event/Harmony hooks. Unconfirmed because pinning a specific currently-exposed channel needs per-channel data-flow tracing. Complements ADV-001/FA-0019 (apply-suppression) from the opposite end.

suspected_unconfirmed.title: Actor DEATH single-railed on reliable tac.damage(0x88) with NO re-convergence backstop
suspected_unconfirmed.detail: On a damage-death the host does registry.Remove(netId) in the ActorDied postfix, so the periodic 0x8F actor-state delta NO LONGER carries the actor, and the 0x8F health mirror explicitly refuses HP <= HealthDeathThreshold ('DEATH is owned by tac.damage'). So the ONLY message that kills the client's mirror is the one-shot 0x88 damage packet; unlike every other actor state, death has NO 0x8F re-assertion backstop. If that single 0x88 is ever lost or applied out-of-context, the client keeps a permanent live ZOMBIE mirror. DEFENDED TODAY: all tactical broadcasts default reliable=true and Steam/Direct are in-order, so 0x88 is not dropped while linked -- below the floor. Real single-rail dependency with the re-convergence path deliberately disabled + no tactical divergence probe (IMP-003).

suspected_unconfirmed.title: TryBuy affordability check skipped if HasResources reflection fails to bind while Take binds
suspected_unconfirmed.detail: Affordability is gated by 'if (resources != null && _walletHasResources != null && !HasResources.Invoke(...)) reject;' then Take.Invoke runs if _walletTake bound. Wallet.Take clamps to available + always returns true, so the pre-check is what rejects an unaffordable buy. If reflection binds _walletTake but NOT _walletHasResources (partial EnsureBuy failure), the pre-check is skipped and an unaffordable client still receives the reward. Requires a partial reflection-bind failure that would likely surface elsewhere too; host-authoritative + offline. Fail-closed fix: reject the buy if _walletHasResources failed to bind.

suspected_unconfirmed.title: ChunkReassembler completion trusts first fragment's TotalLen but concats by received frag lengths -> silent truncate/zero-pad on mismatch
suspected_unconfirmed.detail: Pending.TotalLen is taken from whichever fragment created the set; completion allocates outBuf=new byte[TotalLen] and walks fragments advancing off += d.Length, clamping to Min(d.Length, TotalLen-off). If summed fragment lengths disagree with TotalLen (corruption / version skew) the blob is silently truncated or zero-padded rather than rejected. Happy path never triggers (one Split() -> consistent TotalLen) and a malformed blob fails closed downstream at TacticalDeployCodec.TryDecode -- adversarial/corruption-only. Fix: verify sum(frag lengths)==TotalLen before returning.

suspected_unconfirmed.title: Stale comment on refund floor guard after EffectiveStat reframe (no exploit)
suspected_unconfirmed.detail: 13e203f reframed 'cur' in the stat-refund loop from raw GetBaseStat to EffectiveStat (always > 0 for a living soldier). The guard 'if (cur <= 0) break; // never below the floor' can no longer act as an allocation floor -- it now only fires as a reflection-failure sentinel (EffectiveStat returns 0 when binding fails), a reasonable safety net, so NOT dead. The comment is stale: the real anti-farm floor is the StatRefundTracker per-(unit,stat) ledger, not this guard. NOT exploitable (the ledger caps refunds to session-net-applied). Comment-staleness, not a behavioral defect.

suspected_unconfirmed.title: Chat text not stripped of control chars before host re-broadcast / logging
suspected_unconfirmed.detail: HandleChat re-broadcasts client Text host-stamped without stripping CR/LF or control characters. Rendered in the in-game ChatLog (not a shell/HTML sink) and not obviously written to Debug.Log, so impact is cosmetic/log-forging at most. Needs confirmation whether chat text ever reaches a structured log/aggregator. Length bounded only by the packet frame (SEC-001/FA-0003).

suspected_unconfirmed.title: IntentDedup bounded ring (512): a legit intent evicted before its reliable double-send twin arrives would be re-applied
suspected_unconfirmed.detail: OnActionRequest dedups client intents by (peerId, GeoIntent, nonce) in a 512-entry ring. The reliable transport double-sends every packet; dedup drops the twin. If >512 DISTINCT intents from other peers arrive between an intent's two copies, the first is evicted and the twin is treated as new -> the action applies twice. In practice the two copies arrive near-simultaneously (far fewer than 512 intervening), so this is a theoretical bound, not an observed defect. Evict by age/session or size the ring to the max in-flight window if ever a concern.

suspected_unconfirmed.title: Host 'reject ALL client geoscape intents while a blocking modal is armed' gate released only by a hand-maintained set of modal-close patches
suspected_unconfirmed.detail: Host opens a blocking modal -> Arm(modalType); while armed, OnActionRequest REJECTS every client ActionRequest. Release is match-gated Release(modalType) driven by two hand-added patches (ModalResultCallback + a haven-details Defend special-case). A blocking modal that closes via any path NOT routed through those leaves _armedModalType set -> every subsequent client geoscape intent rejected until the geoscape/session boundary Reset belt fires. Same hand-maintained-allowlist fragility as HIST-002/FA-0040. BOUNDED (boundary Reset un-sticks it) and no un-patched close path was located today -> below the floor. Also a single-slot arm: a second modal's Arm() overwrites the first's type.

suspected_unconfirmed.title: Host forced-reveal deadline vs a client kicked at phase-1 but still holding its curtain
suspected_unconfirmed.detail: The host forced-reveal and AllDone reveal broadcast RevealAll to CURRENT peers only; a client kicked at the phase-1 straggler timeout (IMP-001/FA-0002) is no longer a peer, so it never receives RevealAll and relies solely on its own per-peer self-reveal. Confidence <40 that this is a distinct defect beyond FA-0002 -- the self-reveal belt exists -- but the kicked-then-never-RevealAll interaction was not runtime-verified.

suspected_unconfirmed.title: Client geoscape sim-freeze release relies on ShouldFreeze going false at teardown, not an explicit unpause
suspected_unconfirmed.detail: The V1/V2 client sim-freeze pins Timing.Paused=true every frame while ShouldFreeze (active-session client). Nothing actively sets Paused=false on session end -- release depends on ShouldFreeze returning false so the per-frame re-assert stops, plus the F3 menu-return tearing the geoscape down. Could not confirm a stuck-frozen state on any real exit path (host-leave yanks the client to menu; a fresh load resets Timing), so below the confidence floor.

suspected_unconfirmed.title: One-writer-per-field not yet achieved for pos / AP-WP / health (documented convergence debt)
suspected_unconfirmed.detail: Canon inv.2 'ONE writer per field' is currently NOT met for POSITION (tac.move END 0x83 + spine POS 0x0008 dual-rail), AP/WP (tac.damage 0x88 + spine + ClientApPreserveGate = 3 writers), HEALTH (down/death via 0x88 vs up/heal via spine = split-by-direction). These are the canon's OWN acknowledged convergence targets (design S2 audit table; roadmap Inc4/Inc6) and canon S7 explicitly permits incremental convergence, so NOT fresh violations -- recorded so the one-writer gaps are on the audit trail. Confidence sub-floor because it is sanctioned debt.

suspected_unconfirmed.title: Reserved-but-unencoded actor-state field bits are mild speculative surface, but tracked
suspected_unconfirmed.detail: The 0x8F actor-state mask carries RESERVED bits declared but not yet encoded/decoded/applied: ActorFieldEquip 0x0080 and ActorFieldOverwatch 0x0100 (roadmap Inc6 P-list). Reserved enum bits with no live wiring are a mild YAGNI smell, NOT a defect because they are explicit dated roadmap placeholders with a defined activation increment and are zero-cost. Recorded so a reader does not mistake a reserved bit for a live-but-broken field.

coverage.files_reviewed_note: L3 full audit. Wave1: cli-scanner-csharp (semgrep/dotnet-format/build+2271 tests PASS/gitleaks clean), cli-scanner-universal, waste-scanner (dead-code via Serena+grep), web-researcher (dep/CVE). Wave2: diff-scanner (HEAD~40..HEAD, 77 files), history-reviewer (565 commits/228 fixes), comment-checker, convention-checker (sync-canon). Wave2.5: impact-reviewer (blast-radius) + reproduction-agent (static repro-trace of IMP-001/002/003, HIST-001, CMT-001/CONV-002). Wave3: code-reviewer-security, logic-reviewer, arch-reviewer, adversarial-hunt (12 co-op failure zones). verify-gate re-adjudicated the 5 top findings (SEC-001/002/003/005, LOG-001), all CONFIRMED, none demoted. No game/Steam DLLs on the audit machine -> all runtime findings are static traces (reproduced=skipped_runtime/no).

limitations: No game/Steam runtime or DLLs on the audit machine (Assembly-CSharp, UnityEngine.*, 0Harmony, TFTV, Facepunch all Private=false). Every runtime-dependent finding is static blast-radius/repro-trace reasoning; the stuck-session HIGHs (FA-0002 straggler kick, FA-0004 heartbeat pin) need a live 2-instance run (tools/launch-instance.bat) to observe the in-game hang. Marked reproduced=skipped_runtime, static-confirmed.

Reflection-heavy host-authoritative mod: correctness of nearly every reflected apply (WalletReflection/EventReflection/PersonnelEditReflection/TacticalActorStateSync etc.) depends on runtime binding against game/TFTV types absent here; guard behavior can only be fully confirmed at runtime. The reflection-fragility findings (FA-0013 parent, FA-0006/FA-0049/FA-0015 instances) are latent/version-drift-conditional -- structurally confirmed at HEAD but not runtime-reproducible without a mismatched game assembly.

C# dead-code / SCA CLI tools from versions.lock not installed (per HARD RULES): NuGone, SonarAnalyzer.CSharp, trivy, dotnet-outdated-tool, dotnet-project-licenses. Dead-code analysis is grep+Serena-LSP based; dependency versions inferred from csproj + web (no packages.config/PackageReference -- all deps are local DLL references with Private=false and no version metadata, so automated version tracking is impossible).
Exact versions of game-shipped DLLs (Facepunch.Steamworks.Win64, 0Harmony, Unity modules) and Phoenix Point's Unity engine version cannot be determined from the csproj; PCGamingWiki returned 403.

Web/ASP.NET slices of the audit spec (SQLi/XSS/CSRF/JWT/CORS/EF Core/IDOR-web/container/K8s/HTTP headers/Blazor) are N/A -- offline P2P Unity game mod (net472), no web surface, DB, ORM, HTTP server, or auth/session cookies. The relevant analog (peer-identity binding) IS covered (FA-0001/FA-0005).

net472 alloc-bomb severity (FA-0003/FA-0012/FA-0016/FA-0043) assumes eager `new T[n]` / BinaryReader.ReadBytes allocation (Framework behavior) and that catching OutOfMemoryException does not promptly restore committed memory under sustained spam; `new byte[int.MaxValue]` (~2GB) likely OOMs at the 2GB single-object ceiling, so the DoS relies on mid-range declared lengths allocating repeatedly.

Scope of deep reads: SaveTransferCoordinator (1831 lines) and SyncEngine (2539 lines) read in full/near-full by impact/security agents; TacticalActorStateSync read to ~line 870 for the seq/versioning analysis; comment-checker focused on the sync priority dirs, not all 390 product .cs files for cosmetic drift. History via rtk-proxied raw git (an unproxied count was confirmed inconsistent).

verify-gate re-adjudicated only the 5 tasked findings; the sibling unbounded-alloc findings (FA-0012/0016/0043) and the reproduction-confirmed set (FA-0002/0004/0007/0013) carry their agent/reproduction-adjusted confidences, not an independent second verification.

csharp.md diff-scanner/concurrency checklists are ASP.NET/TPL-tuned and map poorly onto the Unity-main-thread + Steam-callback + coroutine model; the real concurrency risk (transport-callback main-thread-marshalling discipline, FA-0048) is not named by the checklist. 'Canon violation = HIGH minimum' was interpreted as MEDIUM for the two canon-invariant gaps (FA-0020 ordering barrier, CONV-S01 one-writer) because they are the canon's OWN roadmap-tracked incremental-convergence items, not newly-introduced bypasses -- a stricter reading would raise FA-0020 to HIGH.
